channel.title: Comments for page "Guide for Newbies"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Guide for Newbies"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that I have implemented an edit to the previous section about "Leaving Constructive Comments" with a general staff consensus.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
You keep posting these in the wrong place.

Like I said last time, to get feedback and help with your draft, post a board in the Draft Forum, not on random discussion boards.

Please pay attention when people take the time both to speak to you and even link you to where you need to be. This is getting close to spam, and certainly shows you're not taking in the information you're reading.

item.title: Re: My SCP
item.content:encoded:
You have four spaces between the start of line, and [[, thus breaking the wiki syntax.

Whitespace matters when it comes to markup languages.

item.title: My SCP
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: requests for feedback on drafts go in the Drafts and Critique forum. Questions about wikidot coding go in the Questions desk forum.

item.title: Re: SUV
item.content:encoded: I agree. I just wanted to suggest better consistency between the various newbie/rules guides.

item.title: Re: SUV
item.content:encoded: Well, many still do consider it bad form, there's a reason it says that, instead of "forbidden".

item.title: SUV
item.content:encoded: Self-upvoting is mentioned here as "bad form", but it's no longer against the rules. That section should probably be modified/removed to clarify things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this. I'll bring it to staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your first may suck, and that's okay:

I think this subheading from the Tips for Writing tab could use a tweak, as per the "There is no first SCP curse" campaign/mantra/whatever you want to call it.

The rest of that section does a good job of explaining that a first SCP doesn't have to suck, but I can see people reading that boldfaced subheading as "expect your first SCP to suck, so post something half-baked just to get it out of the way, and people will be okay with that because you're new".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should probably be updated with the new team changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original 5000+ post Metathread actually got wiped by accident some time back. It's since been locked because the amount of spam it garnered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The broken link remains. I was rather looking forward to a good read…

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That's almost certainly just any given Admin, Bland.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
As the Contact Staff tab indicates, we don't have a team dedicated to that particular issue.

The closest thing would be the "I am reporting a user (regular member or staff) who has broken site rules OR I am reporting a user for disruptive behavior staff should be aware of." section, which references the Disciplinary Committee.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Hmmmm - where's the "I'm old, crotchety and want to complain about those kids today" team?

edit: And my joke falls flat…

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a new "Contact Staff" tab has been added to this guide. Further revisions may follow; if you have recommendations for fixes, please state them here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't Quikngruvn be listed as inactive since he hasn't posted anything on the site since May?
E: And shouldn't the portion of Clef heading the GOC wiki be removed, since the wiki isn't active anymore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a relic from before we figured out how to disallow automatic signatures. That said, I think it's still valid so as to warn people who manually input signatures. Not just quote stuff, but stuff like literally signing their username at the end of their posts, which has occasionally/rarely happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Signatures: Don't. Signatures are unnecessary and not allowed on the forums.

Given that the site's settings hide forum signatures anyways, is this line necessary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please PM an admin on wikidot or the chat about this. It's respectful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unaware I was supposed to do this through PM's, please disregard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should be fixed now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Closed - **Do not respond to this post. ** Staff is posting a statement that does not require a reply.

This is bolded incorrectly, just wanted to let you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the link. Should work now; thanks for the heads up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed buy Accelerando's link is broken on the Senior Staff tab. That I find strange.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Just noting here that this sentence has been removed:

The only valid reason for upvoting your own article is to counter malicious downvoting, and only with the blessing of a staff member.

as per this discussion on the admin site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Whoops. Either way, both are places that show how not to act (and neither are linked properly).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The page source actually has a link to Thread… which is a pretty good place to show people how not to act on the forums. I have no idea why it's not working.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was a link to Bad Applications which has since been taken out (the top of the page says why). That sentence really ought to be deleted.

e: damnit links

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Under the "Site Behavior" tab regarding Spam: the link that should be in "…go here, read as much as you can physically tolerate, and then never do any of that" is absent…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So has Bright been getting a lot of 'cute' password entries?
Or is he predicting future events?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eric just turned 50, so you should update his age.

item.title: Just a thought...
item.content:encoded:
Maybe we could include descriptions/definitions for casual site lingo/abbreviations, such as Skip, Scip, GOI, etc? Many, many new guys in chat often derail discussion on site topics to explain them.

Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've wondered for many a year…

Why isn't this the Guide For Newbies? Wouldn't a Guide To Newbies be something disseminated to senior staff outlining how to behave towards new people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the link to the bad applications, as that has led many people astray.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a broken link to the metathread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In fact, if you want a general guide to how not to behave on the forums, go here, read as much as you can physically tolerate, and then never do any of that.

…Go where?

item.title: Staff Post: Rewrite
item.content:encoded: As of 8:47 CDT, 17 July 2012, this guide has been rewritten, by me. I used much of the structure and some of the phrasing of the original Kens version, but rewrote ambiguous or outdated parts and added links. This was done with administrative approval; send TroyL a message if you have comments or questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I could swear that I've heard a bunch of people complaining that people were misusing the sandbox that way. Anyways, reverted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reject:

That's not really a rule. You can make a blank sandbox page if you want. (I've done it for experimentation purposes.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Read and found to be very informative. Thanks a bunch.

item.title: Sandbox?
item.content:encoded:
Also, should there maybe be a little segment on how to use the sandbox (i.e. actually posting in the sandbox, not making a special page devoted to an SCP you're working on rather than a page for your work in general, etc.)

EDIT: I went ahead and added that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a link to the guide for -J articles under the appropriate section.

item.title: Re: Newbies Guide Re-write
item.content:encoded: We should update the chat mod list.

item.title: Newbies Guide Re-write
item.content:encoded: Alright, the new version is up. Okay'd by Bright, so hopefully everything's good to go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah. I guess they don't know you don't have to join to read comments:

SCP Forum on the left for things that aren't "Per Page Discussions" and that's even doable if you want to. I think.

Recent Posts gives everything including Per Page Discussions.

The only thing you don't get is threaded "per page" stuff because that part is hidden.

(EDIT: Not like YOU don't know this, this is just general commenting.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The main problem is that I've been seeing is people who join with a join request aproximating 'I don't want to contribute, I just want to be able to read comments'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think everyone can agree that there are a LOT of ways to contribute: writing, editing, fodder images, voting, commenting, chat, projects, scripting, funding, etc. There's a lot of things you can do, and they can all help a community like this.

I think if you join something knowing you're not going to do ANYTHING, though, it's rather pointless, which speaks to your own character.

I'm not sure I agree with the phrasing, however. You can want to join something, but not know how you can best contribute before you get into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think joining just to vote is perfectly acceptable. Contributing to the overall quality of the site by putting in a vote that allows an article to avoid deletion or assists staff in reaching the quota for removal of bad submissions is contributing, in a big way. Frankly, I would rather have a thousand people who vote but never comment than even one who comments without voting.

I'd like to reword that bit further to include voting. For the silent majority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, worded much better, since "contribute" does cover such a broad area. (Personally, I don't have a problem with people wanting to join just to vote on stuff, but I see where the sentiment can come from.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh! Contribute IS posting or doing anything else than just, well lurk. Well I think that wording is a bit better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rephrased it a little, what do people think?
Bright, who does applications, has been seeing applications from people who specifically say they don't want to add anything, and that from that, there isn't a real difference from just not joining the site. "Contributing" is pretty open-ended- personally, for me, I would see joining the site just to vote as being kind of tacky, but leaving good comments as probably alright- but I'm gonna wait for Bright and see if he wants to clarify that, as applications are his deal.
So what it's trying to say is, don't join the site if you don't want to do anything with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well trolls are actually "writting" just saying, but If I had seen that back when I came around, maybe I wouldn't be around anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's in response to the handful of trolls making bullshit articles lately. I don't like the wording of it, though. Maybe "write or otherwise contribute to our community"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was added 14 hours ago, check History.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe it is a left over from when we had applications. No sense processing applications for people who just wanted to read.

Whatever the cause though I don't like that guideline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If people that don't plan to contribute couldn't join there would be a lot less votes in articles me thinks. And contribute here means comment or post articles?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please don't join the site if you don't actually intend to write or contribute.

What brought this on? Is it actually an issue I'm unaware of?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should we link the FAQ page to this?
Or is there a need?
